summary: Independent QA accepts the M1 database schema and seed implementation. Prisma SQLite configuration, MVP models, optional user ownership, development-safe Prisma client helper, seed script, package scripts, env example, and README database commands satisfy the task acceptance criteria.

tests_run.result: Next.js production build completed successfully with the known Windows native SWC fallback warning.

decisions: Accepted the literal file:./dev.db Prisma datasource for this task because it satisfies local SQLite development and README documents a later PostgreSQL provider and URL switch.
Accepted nullable RadarTask.userId as sufficient optional future ownership without a users table.
Confirmed no admin auth, UI CRUD, SERP provider, LLM, or scan orchestration was implemented.
risks: Next.js still reports a local Windows native SWC binary warning before succeeding through fallback behavior.
Unrelated untracked skills/project-relay remains outside this task and should not be staged with M1 work.
requested_action: Proceed with integration gate for M1-DB-SCHEMA-SEED-001 while keeping unrelated skill-tree changes separate.
